commonIssues:
With almost all 13 RTO test tracks automated, over 20% fail due to zero margin for error. High-resolution cameras fail applicants for 1-inch inaccuracies, especially on the 'Figure-8' and 'Straight T' reversing tracks.

How to fix: Practice extensively on similar tight tracks. Ensure your car's mirrors are perfectly angled. If you fail, you must wait 7 days to apply for a retest via the Sarathi portal.

Severe slot shortages at Vasant Vihar/Rohini, portal server issues, and E-KYC Aadhaar address mismatches causing instant rejection.

How to fix: Always update your Aadhaar to your current Delhi address BEFORE applying. Book slots late at night when server traffic is low. If popular RTOs are full, select a less crowded zonal RTO.

NOCs are required to renew out-of-state licenses in Delhi. Licenses expired for over 1 year mandate an entirely new application and re-test.

How to fix: Always apply for renewal within the 30-day grace period. For out-of-state records not visible online (Backlog), you must retrieve a physical NOC from your home state RTO before applying in Delhi.

Under the 5-Challan rule, 5 violations in a year cancel your license. Violations like no helmet/seatbelt lead to immediate on-the-spot suspension. Driving with expired PUC/DL yields ₹10,000 fines.

How to fix: Maintain digital hygiene—use apps to remind you of PUC/Insurance/DL expiry. Drive flawlessly as automated traffic cameras will log challans without police presence.
